response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with nearly two decades of software engineering experience, staff-level titles, and recent hands-on exposure to MCP servers and agentic-workflow tooling, which aligns with parts of the role. However, the position requires 8+ years building production AI-driven solutions, and the resume shows only about one year of AI-adjacent work with no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, Python, evaluation pipelines, or LLM optimization. Strong engineering fundamentals and customer-facing teaching experience partially offset the GenAI gap, warranting a screening conversation rather than a clear yes.
strengths: 19+ years of full-stack engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Current hands-on work with MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure at staff level
Proven mentorship and stakeholder-facing skills through workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8+ years building production AI-driven solutions
No demonstrated experience with RAG, vector databases, evaluation pipelines, or LLM optimization
Python is absent from the skills list despite being a key technology for the role

key_factors.factor: Deep general engineering and staff-level track record
Recent MCP and agentic tooling exposure
